philosophy:
My research aims to advance sustainability in fashion through three interconnected areas: (1) promoting sustainable materials and eco-conscious production; (2) understanding consumption patterns and addressing the wearer's experience; and (3) developing sustainable apparel design techniques through zero-waste patternmaking and modular design.

For empirical research, I adopt qualitative, quantitative, and mixed-methods approaches, depending on the research purpose, to examine how sustainability is perceived, communicated, and adopted. My creative scholarship explores eco-friendly materials and design techniques, including zero-waste patterning, modular clothing, and textile repurposing.

I believe true sustainability must care for people, and this grounds my human-centered design study. My aim is to integrate ergonomic performance, aesthetic expression, and participatory design to create garments that serve diverse body types, mobility needs, and sensory preferences.
